Add DropCore UniFFI object with full protocol API

Exposes a high-level DropCore object via UniFFI proc macros:
- Identity: generate/restore, get device ID and public key
- Peers: add, list, lookup with SQLite persistence
- Messages: compose (encrypt), receive (decrypt + dedup), pending queue
- Bloom filter: build from pending recipients, check against own ID
- Handshake: build/parse for GATT connection negotiation
- Chunking: split messages for MTU, reassemble with state tracking, ACKs
- Error handling: DropError enum (Database, Crypto, InvalidData, PeerNotFound)

All types are FFI-friendly (Vec<u8>, String, records) for Swift/Kotlin.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Wire DropRepository to UniFFI-generated Rust DropCore bindings

Replace all placeholder/TODO implementations in DropRepository with real
calls to the UniFFI-generated DropCore object from the drop-ffi Rust crate.

Key changes:
- Constructor now takes Context for DB path and SharedPreferences access
- Singleton DropCore instance (thread-safe double-checked locking) to avoid
  SQLite locking across multiple DropRepository instances
- Secret key persistence via SharedPreferences (base64-encoded)
- BLE MAC → Drop device ID mapping populated during handshake exchange
- Shared MutableStateFlow state (conversations, messages) across instances
  so BLE service and UI observe the same data
- Chunk reassembly via processChunk → receiveMessage pipeline
- Pending messages split into BLE-MTU-sized chunks via splitIntoChunks
- Graceful error handling with try/catch around all FFI calls

Remaining TODOs:
- parseAck() not yet in FFI — handleAck is a no-op with logging
- Peer registration via addPeer() after public key exchange
- Custom display name support
- Read/unread tracking for conversations
- Message history persistence (currently in-memory only)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add invariant extraction template and protocol (#64)

* Add invariant extraction template and protocol

New components for extracting structured invariants (constraints,
state machines, timing assumptions, ordering rules, error conditions)
from specifications or source code:

- Protocol: invariant-extraction — 6-phase methodology (source
  classification, constraint extraction, state machine extraction,
  error condition extraction, structuring, completeness check)
- Template: extract-invariants — one template with source_type param
  (spec or code), produces requirements-doc with optional state
  machine appendix

Design decisions:
- One template, source_type param switches persona behavior
- Output is requirements-doc format (composable with audit templates)
  plus state machine appendix when state-driven behavior is found
- Positioned as a filtered subset of reverse-engineer-requirements —
  dense constraints only, not comprehensive requirements

Closes #40

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scenario persona, use RFC 2119 keywords for code sources too

- Scenario: persona is specification-analyst (fixed, not switchable)
- Code-sourced invariants now use MUST/SHOULD/MAY in requirement text
  with enforcement status as a separate annotation (e.g.,
  'MUST [enforced via assertion]') for format compliance

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scenario: say 'specification' not 'RFC' to avoid conflict with roadmap

extract-invariants handles generic specs and code. RFC-specific
extraction is a separate template (extract-rfc-requirements) still
listed as a future scenario.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add generate/verify loop: implementation + test prompt templates (#61)

* Add generate/verify loop: implementation + test prompt templates

New personas:
- implementation-engineer — builds code from specs, traces every
  decision to a REQ-ID, enforces constraints explicitly
- test-engineer — writes tests from specs, covers negative cases and
  boundaries, maps every assertion to an acceptance criterion

New templates:
- author-implementation-prompt — spec + design -> structured coding
  brief for a coding agent. Includes REQ-ID traceability instructions.
- author-test-prompt — spec + validation plan -> structured test
  generation brief. Includes TC-NNN traceability instructions.

Both produce requirements-doc format output. Both pair with their
corresponding audit templates (audit-code-compliance and
audit-test-compliance) for a generate/verify loop: same spec drives
the coding prompt and the audit prompt, so they can't drift.

Closes #59

fix: make rerun research respect configured row count across pipeline

Root cause: QA filter cascade (blockers, Jaccard similarity, relevance scoring)
could reduce output to as few as 2 rows with no safety floor. Especially severe
when AI fallback mode produces lower-diversity candidates.

Changes:
- Add progressive QA relaxation: when row count drops below 30% of target,
  progressively relax relevance scoring (25→15→0), then similarity thresholds
  (0.7→0.85→skip, 0.65→0.8→skip), ensuring minimum output guarantee
- Fix rerun mode: always send mode='fresh' (no uploaded file available on rerun)
- Expand fallback cluster diversity: 3→10 base templates per language
- Filter metadata categories from pain point pillar fallback
- Add comprehensive pipeline logging (raw rows, QA output, target vs actual)
- Add 10 regression tests covering row floor, targetRows, progressive relaxation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: enforce 90% branch coverage as correctness gate (#46)

* docs: add code coverage research and strict branch coverage spec

Research Rust code coverage tooling (cargo-llvm-cov, grcov, tarpaulin)
and create a spec for enforcing 90% branch coverage as a correctness
gate for agentic code generation.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* chore: add coverage artifacts to .gitignore

Add ignore patterns for coverage/, lcov.info, *.profraw, and
*.profdata to prevent coverage artifacts from being committed.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* ci: add codecov.yml with 90% branch coverage targets

Configure Codecov to enforce 90% coverage on both the overall
project and on new code in PRs (patch coverage). Excludes test
files, research, and specs from coverage metrics.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* ci: add 90% branch coverage gate with cargo-llvm-cov

Add a parallel coverage job to CI that uses nightly Rust and
cargo-llvm-cov to enforce 90% branch coverage as a correctness
gate for agentic code generation.

- Nightly toolchain with llvm-tools-preview for branch coverage
- Node.js 22 so scaffold E2E tests run (no silent skips)
- Merged profraw from workspace tests + doctests
- Hard gate via --fail-under-branches 90
- Codecov upload with if: always() for PR annotations
- Existing ci job (build/test/clippy/fmt) unchanged

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* docs: add mandatory coverage commands to CLAUDE.md

Add a Coverage Commands section that instructs LLM agents to
run 90% branch coverage checks before pushing. Includes commands
for threshold enforcement, HTML reports, and lcov generation
for editor gutters, plus toolchain prerequisites.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* ci: exclude test/research/specs from coverage threshold

Add --ignore-filename-regex to cargo-llvm-cov report commands so
the 90% branch coverage gate measures only production code, not
test harness files. Aligns the CI hard gate with codecov.yml's
ignore list to prevent test code from inflating the percentage.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* ci: address PR review comments

- Add permissions: contents: read (security best practice)
- Pin Node.js to 22.6.0 (scaffold tests need >= 22.6 for
  --experimental-strip-types)
- Gate Codecov upload on CODECOV_TOKEN availability so fork
  PRs don't fail when the secret is missing
- Set fail_ci_if_error: false so Codecov outages don't block
  merges (the --fail-under-branches gate is the hard check)

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* Update .github/workflows/ci.yml

* Update .github/workflows/ci.yml

* fix: replace nonexistent --fail-under-branches with script

cargo-llvm-cov does not have a --fail-under-branches flag.
Parse the TOTAL line from the text report and compare the
branch percentage against 90% using bc. Also update CLAUDE.md
to remove the invalid flag from local commands.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* fix: use awk instead of bc for branch coverage comparison

bc may not be available on all runners. awk is universally
available and handles floating-point comparison natively.
Also verified the full coverage pipeline works locally:
current branch coverage is 80.72%.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* refactor: introduce Fs trait for filesystem testability

Add a filesystem abstraction trait (fs::Fs) with 4 methods:
exists, create_dir_all, write_file, read_to_string. Production
code uses fs::Real which delegates to std::fs. Tests can inject
mock implementations to simulate I/O errors and reach branch
coverage on all ? error paths without touching the real filesystem.

Refactored modules:
- init.rs: init() now takes &dyn Fs
- workspace_init/mod.rs: init(), init_workspace(), scaffold_marketplace()
- workspace_init/adaptors/claude.rs: ToolAdaptor::apply() takes &dyn Fs
- aipm/main.rs and aipm-pack/main.rs: pass &fs::Real

All existing tests pass — they use fs::Real for real filesystem.

Assistant-model: Claude Code

* test: add branch coverage tests across all modules

Add tests targeting 32 missed branches to reach 90% coverage:

Phase 1: Quick wins
- 5 scoped-name edge cases in init.rs (invalid_names)
- Multi-error format test for error.rs (format_errors)
- select_best None + is_prerelease false for version.rs

Phase 2: Moderate effort
- No-subcommand E2E test for aipm-pack
- 3 malformed JSON tests for claude.rs (invalid, non-object, bad value)
- Preconfigured settings test for workspace_init (adaptor false path)

Phase 3: Mock Fs I/O error paths
- FailFs + CountingFs mocks in init.rs for create_dir/write_file errors
- FailDirFs + FailWriteFs mocks in workspace_init for I/O errors

Coverage: 80.72% -> 86.21% (174 branches, 24 missed)
Assistant-model: Claude Code

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design
